textByHash.02183756c120a8d5cb9d5e684da61a264addf437: Hmmm, which runes would you think are sticky.
textByHash.0291fee89bd0ae6811cecfbd68cf4433a38a899f: Are you really interested in all of this? Do you want to see something interesting?
textByHash.053782a6dd40ac67adddeb499027381daf2116ea: Oh my goodness. I think I'm beginning to understand where you are going with this.
textByHash.07af9ef9f26dc1e16c99d54e20ce5cdfb8f9c604: Ah, thank you my young apprentice. What did he tell you?
textByHash.085bd32585ac5cf063dcad72cad61dc86cbfddc0: Excellent, it's so rare I can find someone to discuss my researches, most gnomes seem to find it all very tedious.
textByHash.0a40a3b59b0b066ed32548049663f77c35a026fa: Fine! Humans really do prefer to live their lives in ignorance...
textByHash.0c05aa414199c5090878b3d5d4597e679d0d36a3: Invisibility is a form of illusion magic. So that makes sense...
textByHash.0d61dc726883e2661f39c022bc5a20b07e78697c: Well, maybe? I'm not so sure. As it happens I've been looking into that part of our History. All I've managed to establish was that he was particularly interested in optics and light magic.
textByHash.0d73ed9fe06c2e003ffa278a447c3e4d8d741241: Oh, that's not my potplant. I think it was Oaknock's.
textByHash.11f58ce313005975a22bfe0b76ea2a7d8253d758: So what do you think the number means?
textByHash.122d61ff2f79e3b003a2b31aec6d8186420397ef: Oaknock was alive and might have seen him use it.
textByHash.1603fd5a7a849719db6c426e417fc810d6662e6d: I have no idea. But I do know someone who might.
textByHash.1784fa72a734e64760c6cbe18a955fdba4e71348: That's the spirit! This is all good training for the mind...
textByHash.17eb4e395dc14b9c999e1299628d5755db3082d6: Alright. Someone snuck in here without us noticing. How?
textByHash.1980060b97d7c9ec33f5074d359b595a40ad67a1: You have my gratitude, young delivery person!
textByHash.2394909e93676de42c06774f5fa8e6f9ef85d530: I wonder if the different sizes and shapes mean anything.
textByHash.28bfea9dcf0ae8c69ed376c66ee06307c5ebbaed: Fascinating ... a crystal seed. You should have a look at the crystal bowl in the other room, must be related...
textByHash.29155bc0eeba0cb5cab06d0896f39f8ff4473fb3: Oaknock the Engineer and Glouphrie didn't like each other either...
textByHash.2b8d371cb830243b96251bf71ee1b6a50f1f33de: Most gnomes don't really talk about him. Other than telling stories about him as if he was some sort of boogie-man.
textByHash.3086a9444b566d17e2001013fad61f9a1821f23d: Izzie just wandered into my cave. He was cute and friendly so I kept him.
textByHash.31fc2c2e11f9ae604b0762dde6fa9e8cbcd9a244: There is one gnome left who knew Oaknock, and King Healthorg and Glouphrie for that matter...
textByHash.324a907571e38a71ac51f5a48775c5ff8a5ca246: So haven't you managed to have a chat with Hazelmere yet?
textByHash.34075a4d0734e541774ef1d6ce32c0738ac65337: I'm not sure. He's very cute though don't you think? It's very restful having a pet, especially when you are stuck in a cave for as long as I am!
textByHash.3b8690c48e93dd9b52febf817fcd3102ce0e6f30: Ah, Oaknock was an engineer and architect. Very famous. There are statues to him around the place - his reputation amongst gnomes is second only to King Healthorg himself. He built most of the Stronghold you know.
textByHash.3e1c1ed79172b9a414af090f08f15803bf400a68: Are you a budding mage? I can tell you more about my research if you'd like.
textByHash.3ed18cca9e0de514e308c29eb15aff8d20491257: Gah! Young mages these days. No patience. No perseverance. Magic is not a toy or a shiny weapon you know! You cannot alter reality unless you understand it. And you cannot understand unless you search for the true causes of things. And history is a record of the causes of things.
textByHash.412f1739a8abb8fb0fd41f870a9a1ec54a62952b: Wait a minute, how did the saboteur know about the machine?
textByHash.4920cbd85021bd5ba0fbf7ab9483aa974167b47d: Of course. Which makes it imperative that we fix this machine and find out what it is!
textByHash.4ac2b384ac85c56a7f8d429d23f9c2f6e2c31236: Is it a solid, a liquid or a gas?
textByHash.4eba219825b214bce45ef3467628eb36c41bec7c: Well, I'm not really sure. This was all before I was born - and no-one talks about what happened to Glouphrie these days.
textByHash.4ee44e6896fa801c1ca13ed66291b9a483da99b8: Right. And why would they sabotage an anti-illusion device?
textByHash.542942fb95f6684d2b2f702364b886b6ca615702: I think King Narnode has one, and I think I saw one by the gates. And I'm sure I've seen a few more wandering around.
textByHash.5740761bbe0d98592129f0d0e7622d01c8866ddb: Well there is a bit of history there, do you want me to tell you about it?
textByHash.5aa3ff7148ebac1834a8cb54bde6b82e1805d206: If you are really interested in all of this, then could you do me a favour and visit Hazelmere for me? I've been meaning to go for a while, but I have responsibilities here - there's always someone who wants to use the Rune Essence mines.
textByHash.60ea27e2d67be80af0ad1839be56da8f57a52052: Well done ! I knew you'd work it out. I'll make a mage out of you yet...
textByHash.619aee9a77361eddcf114eb6f061f36e10c28caf: They are not quite the same as the one Hazelmere gave you, but they look similar in size and weight.
textByHash.648b09c456b603756bc9c894bb47c00cb9e011b7: Oh, I've been rooting through my drawers and I've found a few more discs that you might find useful.
textByHash.6515143dadf6f6553f255833392e9051979f4a5b: That's right. He was the third advisor to King Healthorg, he found and tended to the Grand Tree.
textByHash.6a4b14c8159c02e92bc6e1aac2b805420b875bbd: Don't you know anything? [sigh] A glamour is a type of enchantment or magical effect.
textByHash.6b73403b79e02e7c6afd12f8a958d5dcf51970d3: Isn't that obvious? I investigate Thaumaturgy, I research the Arcane, I attempt to explain the inexplicable.
textByHash.6c2f436db19554e2554ce1f7eeae94f2ca460716: Well that's true. These days, apart from a few combat spells most gnomes seem to be a bit suspicious of mages.
textByHash.6dd5685e8d79383d4c95975559daa3317f71abc1: Well, I'd better carry on with my research then..
textByHash.708f877d4e744aa743077103f14ff2fccaf59752: So this horrible thing is listening to us right now... Aargh. Get rid of it! Don't forget to tell the king what's going on!
textByHash.75827a6d0d30da1a9cbb0db5dac1e76379d9907d: Well, indeed. I have also found though he was generally disliked by other gnomes - his moniker was 'Glouphrie the Untrusted'.
textByHash.7bd403852cc2250f1d27058fd070343e35274e6b: It's always a pleasure to catch up with an another mage! Especially one who has helped me so much!
textByHash.7eaecfd2a78567c7cf1607b6ac67132d9084b2f1: Errm, I'm not sure. Have a chat with Hazelmere, maybe he'll know. He was around when
textByHash.7f2498aa24424806614d8285406d1830e0e0b80c: Very wise, a good knowledge of the History of Magic is crucial to understanding the Arcane.
textByHash.7fd1c0f8bbbcdf2b966800411286f67e90591481: Who knows, but he may well have taught the skills to his gnomes.
textByHash.8059a5567f3370bfbfad52a5bacb5f8b8ea5a9bd: I wonder how it works? a similar way to the locking mechanism I'd guess.
textByHash.82e035fdc22fc4254e34d3002c883afb99f5e25c: So there you go. What more do you need to know?
textByHash.8d2882f41caf65d69530fefb7bf93e8a0d8678a3: He seemed very curious, and used to watch me do my researches. I liked the companionship – research is lonely work...
textByHash.92a560558305876c2441250a8655ecd279fb291d: Well, it seems that Izzie was not so cute after all! How awful! How mysterious!
textByHash.93d19f2e1603baeeb616fe11c156b1312c685031: Right, and how does one make oneself invisible my boy?
textByHash.95f3ce23ab719d5318215ad630edff4f031b3b8a: Well maybe if you had, you'd know something about tree sap!
textByHash.96b8e3ff9083505fb80215461a47227563302a5b: Ha! He's an old fashioned kind of gnome and doesn't like visitors much. But I'm sure he'll want to talk about the Old Days.
textByHash.97c4f42e1d1c6b44ec56c57e36aeb7b30c50607f: Hello adventurer, what can I do for you?
textByHash.98900def36f19b2fbb944eb26a25d48d2f609192: So where do you put liquids?
textByHash.9a13b7a44f15e854a129bc41b9139ff1f2e9d34e: That room used to be Oaknock's workshop. I'd also recognise his style anywhere.
textByHash.9ee7e65c15181b758acab3e1066269201a6ef86b: Open your mind and think laterally. Thinking sideways is an important skill for a mage.
textByHash.9fe3b9b1b32586c1c9895f411b771b984aba7a61: Oh, by the way, I found this odd thing lying around, does it belong to you?
textByHash.a25478b8f27cd41aebdc8c446448ee7967f04e82: I suppose it must be. It shakes every now and again you know... I don't know why.
textByHash.a2de539b660025d1e51209275ebe2f2db569932c: Right! I think you know everything you need now...
textByHash.a656f50559016991c8402b0b16055a350d8a6650: Hmmm, use your head! What kind of substance is this tree sap?
textByHash.a8d2e5fd6504b14f697644e0648f15d786ece63e: Indeed, sadly I know very little about that, it looks elven to me though...
textByHash.a8dc59bb7b3d68a3706dc78e410c85f9da009eeb: I suppose it has. I hadn't thought of it like that. I'm sure I've seen others of these creatures around the place...
textByHash.aa3eab301cbd155ade3201c10faf5e56818fb7eb: Really? Wait - what's that banging noise?
textByHash.ac2017eb19723d4412082c11e136daed7239df7a: Well, have a careful look around at everything in the next cave and come back and tell me what you find.
textByHash.ac7e07e1cdc4a6e88cc9ee2b1b4d02918a3c7616: Ridiculous! He was a gnome like any other - he sounds like a fascinating character.
textByHash.ad5522300b2cadfae5975d2f3c886b61b4da2384: Have you had a look at the damage yet?
textByHash.b3a68d4be46d9e0350e4a1d664faf5390ad3e4d0: Well done! You're almost as good at fixing things as a gnome! Who'd have thought?
textByHash.b3c903804bc8b4a65b9c9c4465cd53d5da26196f: But we were just standing here, how could someone get past us?
textByHash.b447762a67b1677cd79cfedca82c6b56919998ae: Have you talked to the King yet?
textByHash.b65a3f0be62e9187d3d3af4748a0bd9f9e68eaff: It smelt of pine and was ambery in colour, that sounds like tree sap. From a coniferous tree probably.
textByHash.b7eab80bb91a72eb79c31ea144a0f02f3aeca9b0: That's right, use your noddle, exercise your noggin, employ your cranium. Think, think, think!
textByHash.b8fb8b9ba2e43e74dc527a35853bf6299aff65c8: Izzie! No! There's something very wrong going on here...
textByHash.bc45930c724c025b79d7dd2fb164fbd7151bea90: He was one of King Healthorg the Great's advisors during the Grand Migration. But he was exiled after the Goblin Wars.
textByHash.c21a0e095897308a67853975937abfc46434ed6e: That may be true, though he may just be faking it to deter visitors. Either way - he is a wise old gnome. If he really wants to talk to you I'm sure he'll find a way.
textByHash.c92b0dd3c645becabf008ca9f54ee5f4a52f3f9f: So that horrible thing was listening to us all along. You'd better tell the King what's going on.
textByHash.c94e4723a232e44eea28bc8c1e5457f1d37d9ef8: That's right. It was built by Oaknock.
textByHash.cb90cce7584d6c998d387b2d2a0f3d6a000b133b: Have you never tried cutting a coniferous tree and watching the sap drip out?
textByHash.ccdfbec42562f694ac78d8e976e752025fcd23d8: Do you think the 10th squad did this?
textByHash.d035d281716c270559a7805f32ad5cf2f7a2bbc8: This is intolerable! How could this happen?
textByHash.d47714eb296317d001196bd253974b61aefa53fc: Hmmm. Maybe all of these discs all have different values and you have to choose the right one in order for it to work?
textByHash.d9848dd8813e92575d65274475cf782bf15c8505: I've no idea, it must have been Oaknock's, he liked talking to the elves.
textByHash.db52923c626bbadea681649c4c301921b04b1144: That reminds me, I found those crystal discs I mentioned before.
textByHash.de8272b7365e7c7c5396994dfda83a2a2d15f874: So how are your investigations going?
textByHash.df79ca703da02226b1c80c60bc455b5011fc19be: Patience young apprentice. You are on the right path - just think it through and I'm sure it will become obvious.
textByHash.df8a66fdfd5cc0e399086bde0f82d56b2f6b4ead: So, what do you know so far?
textByHash.e362c4f509dca1d632674ec8113ba13c61bccdda: Right, and how does one make oneself invisible my girl?
textByHash.e3c69672eef2f7848fc2c404672274b36bd68486: Well ask him if he knows anything about Oaknock's machine. And anything you can find about Glouphrie would be very interesting, wouldn't you agree?
textByHash.e4bd2b1a55214b54ccc3856c645836b67bf6eb64: That's a relief. Well, that's all good work, but I think you'd better inform the King now.
textByHash.e9ffba643aeb8bc8b3a760f0a89e9ac6fae84699: Surely this couldn't be the work of Hazelmere... That turncoat!!
textByHash.e9ffd7d106e4c80fd11da467811fecb892f3ddd1: Ah, yes indeed. Well I do have a little mystery for you. Has your curiosity been piqued?
textByHash.edff2cd26d0479da4b0ec116ef6d2b6c39ca0684: So the machine emits an anti-illusion glamour?
textByHash.eed673d10c4a38ee7d68cb73b425889aaf4abc6f: Hmmm ... You're the only person I've told about the machine...
textByHash.f00115a9f23f6bbf8d8f56e9a2b86f24cfd770a6: Have a look at the machine and assess the damage. I'll hunt around my boxes and see if I can find anything that might help.
textByHash.f14be2c1f26a4afd56eb9136f8b63a174d787d1e: You should look after your things more carefully you know...
textByHash.f22e96dce2c58f57eb93daaf004f6a251898e5eb: I'm not sure, but there will be some logic somewhere. Maybe if you look around the workshop you'll find some clues?
textByHash.f43123410f1d9e533ae4d6cd8dc5350d28b7d1b6: Well, King Narnode tells me you've already visited him...
textByHash.f87a828573405ae7c699c1ba5524408f4ca4741a: Oh, crystal discs? I may have a few of those in a drawer somewhere.
textByHash.f89325df606b592a1f54e13f9947ac17b2c5e109: What's happened to the machine? It's broken!
textByHash.ff62f2aafaaa1839ac4f71fa2b32ef52092ed08b: He has a hut in a small island east of Yanille. It has a lovely sea-view, I'm jealous. I love my cave, but it's not very picturesque.
textByHash.ff815b1bef3acc48877f5dffe8dc08c5553d30db: Well, the most famous Gnomish Mage of all got into a bit of .... trouble.
